name: llama3.2
description: Meta's Llama 3.2 is a fast, compact multilingual model well-suited to everyday chat, summaries, and translation. It runs comfortably on a single consumer GPU and is the best starting point for most new users.

name: llama3.1
description: Meta's Llama 3.1 offers stronger reasoning and instruction-following than 3.2, making it a solid mid-range choice when accuracy matters more than raw speed. Supports function calling for agentic use cases.

name: qwen2.5
description: Alibaba's Qwen 2.5 is a general-purpose model with exceptional multilingual coverage spanning 29 languages and strong analytical reasoning. It scales from 7 B to 72 B parameters, offering a size for every GPU budget.

name: qwen2.5-coder
description: A specialised code model from Alibaba's Qwen 2.5 family, optimised for code generation, debugging, and code review across 92 programming languages. It supports repo-level context and is a strong alternative to GitHub Copilot for local use.

name: qwen3
description: Alibaba's third-generation Qwen model with a hybrid thinking mode that can toggle between fast answers and deep step-by-step reasoning. It delivers top-tier quality across coding, maths, and multilingual tasks.

name: qwen3-coder
description: Alibaba's flagship coding model combining Qwen 3's hybrid reasoning with deep software engineering capability. Designed for autonomous coding agents that can plan, write, and debug entire projects.

name: qwen3.5
description: An updated mid-size Qwen model from Alibaba that improves on Qwen 3 in instruction-following and nuanced reasoning. Available in a very large MoE configuration offering frontier-level quality.

name: phi4
description: Microsoft's Phi-4 is a 14 B model that punches well above its weight for structured reasoning and STEM tasks. Its small footprint makes it easy to run locally while still competing with models several times larger.

name: phi4-mini
description: Microsoft's Phi-4 Mini is a compact 3.8 B model that fits on nearly any hardware with 4 GB of VRAM. Surprisingly capable for everyday questions, short writing tasks, and simple code.

name: gemma3
description: Google's Gemma 3 is a lightweight open model series released under a permissive licence. It performs well on coding and instruction tasks and supports a 128 K context window in the larger variants.

name: mistral
description: Mistral 7B is a fast, efficient open-weight model from Mistral AI. It's a great all-rounder for modest hardware and excels at concise answers, classification, and structured extraction.

name: ministral-3
description: Mistral's compact model — at 3 B parameters it runs on almost any laptop and still handles simple Q&A, drafting, and classification tasks reliably. The 8 B variant adds noticeably stronger reasoning.

name: devstral-2
description: Mistral's Devstral 2 is a 24 B model purpose-built for software engineering agents that browse code repositories, execute tools, and iterate autonomously. Strong on complex multi-file edits.

name: devstral-small-2
description: A smaller, faster variant of Mistral's Devstral 2 designed to run on consumer-grade GPUs while retaining strong coding and tool-use capabilities.

name: deepseek-r1
description: DeepSeek R1 is a reasoning-focused model that externalises its chain-of-thought before answering. It shines on maths, logic puzzles, and complex multi-step problems where showing the working matters.

name: deepseek-v3.1
description: DeepSeek V3.1 is a 671 B mixture-of-experts model competitive with frontier closed-source models. It combines strong code generation, multilingual fluency, and long-context understanding.

name: deepseek-v3.2
description: An updated release of DeepSeek's flagship MoE model with improvements to instruction-following, function calling, and coding accuracy. Still runs as a single MoE deployment at 671 B total parameters.

name: gpt-oss
description: OpenAI's open-weight models distributed through Ollama. Useful for experimenting with OpenAI-trained architectures fully offline.

name: cogito
description: Deep Cogito's large reasoning model with strong chain-of-thought performance on complex analytical tasks. Available at frontier scale for demanding workloads.

name: glm-4
description: Zhipu AI's GLM-4 is a bilingual model with particularly strong Chinese and English fluency. It handles long documents, structured extraction, and code well at 9 B parameters.

name: glm-4.5
description: An updated generation of Zhipu AI's GLM family with improved reasoning and instruction-following over GLM-4. Suitable for Chinese-language document analysis and bilingual agent tasks.

name: nemotron-3-nano
description: NVIDIA's Nemotron-3 Nano is a compact model optimised for energy efficiency on NVIDIA hardware. Designed for on-device inference where power and memory are constrained.

name: nemotron-3-super
description: NVIDIA's Nemotron-3 Super is a large reasoning model targeting high-quality STEM and coding benchmarks while leveraging NVIDIA's training infrastructure.

name: kimi-k2
description: Moonshot's Kimi K2 is a massive 1-trillion-parameter mixture-of-experts model built for complex reasoning, long-context understanding, and agentic tasks. Requires significant hardware.

name: nomic-embed-text
description: Nomic's embed-text model produces high-quality text embeddings for semantic search and retrieval. Used by Lumogis as the default embedding model for file and document search.
